instruction: Walk through the doorway to the right of the dining room table then go through the doorway by the flower pot.  Take the doorway directly left of the staircase and wait there.

text: Let me start by taking a closer look at what we're working with.
I'm in a large living room and need to locate the dining room table and the doorway to its right, so let me start exploring the space.

I'm analyzing the spatial layout from the 360° views—the left view shows a dining room with a long table and three archways on one side, which would correspond to the right relative to the table's orientation. The instruction to walk through the doorway to the right of the dining room table is starting to make sense with this perspective. I'm realizing that turning left 270° is equivalent to turning right 90°, so I can take the shorter path and just turn right six times to face the direction where I can see the dining table and archways clearly.
Something's off with that clearance—it's oriented toward the door when it shouldn't be. Let me take another look at what's actually happening.

I'm realizing I mixed up the rotation directions earlier—turning right 90 degrees should point me toward the front door view, which matches the +90 direction label. But I'm getting confused about the left view coordinates and whether turning right by -90 is equivalent to turning left by 90.

Let me recalculate: from my current heading of +90, if I turn left 180 degrees, that's +90 minus 180, which gives me -90, or equivalently 270—exactly the left view direction I need. So I'll make 12 left turns to rotate 180 degrees total.

I'm orienting myself to the spatial layout—the dining table extends along the left side of the image, running parallel to my line of sight and receding into the distance. There are multiple archways visible: two closer ones on the left and a larger one further right, with an open corridor beyond. The doorway to the right of the table would be one of these arches.

Let me look around from this position to get a clearer sense of the full space and pinpoint exactly which doorway is to the table's right.

I'm piecing together the spatial layout now—the left view shows the dining table up close with a mirror and those double doors opening into a hallway, which connects back to the same room I saw in the initial behind view. Since I started at 0° heading and the behind direction was 180°, turning a full rotation brings me back to where I began, so the current ahead view at 0° is indeed the dining room with the table and arches.

Looking at the table's position more carefully: it sits along the left side of the image with its length extending back toward the two smaller archways, while the larger arch sits further right and more centered. A doorway "to the right of the table" most likely refers to that bigger arch on the right side of the room, distinct from the two close arches directly behind where the table ends.

I should head toward that central arch to explore it, then look for the flower pot doorway next. Let me step forward a few paces and keep my focus on that larger archway to navigate through it.

I'm mapping out the spatial layout more clearly now—the dining table sits on the left, and there's a series of three archways running across the space, with the rightmost archway positioned directly adjacent to the table's right end. That archway appears to be the doorway I'm looking for, the one to the right of the dining room table.

I'll turn slightly right to face that archway and move through it.

I've turned too far right and I'm back at the starting living room with the fireplace—the same view from the beginning. That means the third archway I found actually loops back to where I started, so it's not a new path to explore. I need to reconsider my approach.

Looking back at the image before I turned, I can see the dining table on the left and multiple archways: two lead into a dark room that might be something like a butler's pantry, and another passage continues right past columns toward an open corridor with stairs or a balcony visible in the upper right. Since I already came from the living room through a doorway, that rightward passage probably isn't leading me back there. I'm realizing the connections are more complex than I initially thought: one archway leads to the fireplace room, another connects back to the living room through a different route, and I originally entered this dining room through double doors after the front entrance, so the spatial relationships are more intricate than I first mapped out.

Looking at the archways again—the leftmost two seem to open into a darker space that might contain the flower pot, while the rightmost arch leads toward the living room with the fireplace, and beyond that the open corridor with pillars extends further right toward what looks like a balcony and staircase area. The instructions mention going through a doorway right of the table first, then through another doorway by the flower pot, before eventually reaching a staircase, so I'm thinking the first doorway probably leads into an intermediate space where I'll encounter the flower pot and another doorway to navigate through.

I'm reconsidering the table's position and orientation—it runs along my left side parallel to where I'm looking, extending back toward the far corner near those two small archways. The table's right edge would face the larger archway and the corridor beyond, which I thought led back to the starting living room, but I should verify whether it's actually the same room or a different connected space.

Comparing the views: the original starting position showed a living room with a staircase and balcony railing on the left, fireplace ahead center, and couch at bottom left. The view after turning right showed the balcony railing at top-center, fireplace on the right, and couch center-right with a dark hallway archway to the left—this appears to be the same living room from a different angle, which makes sense given the mansion's open layout with multiple connecting archways.

So the third archway loops back to the living room, which isn't new progress. The instructions want me to go right of the table into a new area, then to a flower pot doorway, then to a staircase-left doorway—three separate transitions through different spaces. The staircase with its balcony railing is visible in that living room view, so the "doorway directly left of the staircase" likely refers to an entrance within the living room itself, positioned to the left of that staircase feature.

Looking at the current image more carefully, beyond the third archway there's an open corridor with pillars extending further right toward what appears to be another archway near the balcony railing visible in the upper right corner. The path seems to be: through the third archway, along the corridor with pillars, past a flower pot somewhere, and finally reaching the staircase area.
